---
title: Pixi tasks
---

Pixi tasks are named commands defined by a repository. They hide details such
as the Quarto working directory, profile arguments, environment selection, or
pre-render setup.

## Inspect available tasks

Start with the repository documentation, then inspect `pixi.toml` or the
`[tool.pixi.tasks]` entries in `pyproject.toml`. Run a documented task with:

```bash
pixi run <task-name>
```

Some documentation uses the equivalent abbreviation `pixi r <task-name>`.

## Frequent task roles

| Role | Typical task names | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Local authoring | `preview` | Watch source changes and serve a local preview |
| Final build | `render`, `render-all` | Produce one or all rendered outputs |
| Cleanup | `clean` | Remove previously generated material before rebuilding |
| Setup | `github-auth`, font or package fetch tasks | Provide project-specific prerequisites |
| Validation | figure, download, or script checks | Detect broken referenced resources |

## Examples of project-specific tasks

The structural biology site defines checks for referenced figures, downloads,
and PyMOL scripts:

```bash
pixi run check-figures
pixi run check-downloads
pixi run check-pymol-scripts
```

A notebook-producing project may instead render several profiles and rename or
copy outputs for use in Colab. Such helpers are not general Quarto commands;
use them only in the repository where they are configured.

::: {.callout-warning title="Do not infer task arguments"}
`solution`, `instructor`, and `master` can all name an answer-bearing output
in different projects. Read the task definition before running a profiled
build or documenting its results.
:::
